name: Aves Golf
about: Aves Golf is a state-of-the-art indoor golf training facility that combines cutting-edge technology with personalized coaching to elevate players' games. The facility is equipped with premium Trackman iO launch monitors and PuttView technology, providing data-driven insights for swing and putting refinement. They offer a range of services including individual lessons, club fitting, tune-ups, and a pro shop, making it suitable for golfers of all skill levels looking to improve their performance through advanced tools and expert guidance.
why_golfers_like_it: Trackman iO provides real-time 3D data and spin axis measurements for precise swing analysis
Personalized coaching based on individual body mobility and physical capabilities
PuttView technology offers an interactive 360° putting platform for green refinement
Premium club fitting and tuning services tailored to each player's unique needs
Flexible monthly membership options with tiered access levels

food_and_drink.notes: No food service mentioned on website
No alcohol service mentioned on website

simulator_hardware.notes: Purpose-built indoor simulator with radar, infrared and high-speed imaging technology
360° putting solution with projector and tracking unit for indoor and outdoor use

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't specify whether club rentals are available, but it mentions services like club tune-ups and club fitting, suggesting they may provide club-related equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food or drink service on the website, and no BYOB policies are specified, suggesting outside food may not be permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website doesn't provide specific information about 18-hole play times, as the facility focuses on swing practice, lessons, and technology-driven training rather than full game simulation.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the website specifically mentions 'Junior Lessons' with 5-pack and 10-pack lesson packages, indicating they cater to younger golfers.
